perturbation.original_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owne r - Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already d isclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner
perturbation.changed_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owner
perturbation.explanation: By omitting '- Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already disclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner', we create a conflict with the 'INDEMNIFICATION' section. If the Recipient uses information rightly received from a Third party with no duty of confidentiality and a claim arises, it is unclear if indemnification applies since those exceptions are no longer explicitly defined, even though the Recipient might assume they are protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION The parties agree to defend, indemnify and hold harmless the other party and its officers, directors, agents, affiliates, distributors, representatives and employees from any and all third party clai ms, demands, liabilities, cost and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, costs and expenses resulting from the indemnifying party’s material breach of any duty, representation or warranty as per this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text listed several exceptions to what is considered 'Confidential Information'. The changed text removes these exceptions, specifically the one regarding information rightfully received from a third party. The 'INDEMNIFICATION' section states that the parties will indemnify each other from claims arising from a breach of the agreement. With the exceptions removed, if the Recipient uses information rightfully received from a third party (which was previously allowed) and a claim arises, it's unclear if the Recipient is now in breach and therefore subject to indemnification, creating a direct conflict and impacting enforceability because the definition of confidential information is now significantly narrower.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient is to dispose of paperwork, data, discs, memory cards, e -mails or any copies of modifications made upon previous written ascent f rom the Owner in a manner prescribed by the Owner’s disposal of information policy. The Recipient shall be informed on the protocols of discarding or disposing of any tangible medium upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is to dispose of paperwork, data, discs, memory cards, e -mails or any copies of modifications made upon previous written ascent f rom the Owner in a safe and legal manner.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'in a manner prescribed by the Owner’s disposal of information policy. The Recipient shall be informed on the protocols of discarding or disposing of any tangible medium upon request.' This omission creates a contradiction with the 'RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' clause. Without specific disposal instructions from the Owner, the Recipient might dispose of information instead of returning it as demanded, leading to a breach.
perturbation.location: PROTECTION OF INTEREST . DISPOSAL OF INFORMATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other material (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Recipient to dispose of information according to the Owner's policy and protocols. The changed text only requires disposal in a 'safe and legal manner,' removing the obligation to follow the Owner's policy. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' clause, which mandates the Recipient to 'immediately deliver' all materials to the Owner on demand. The modified disposal clause now allows for disposal of information (in a safe and legal manner), which directly contradicts the requirement to return all information to the Owner, thus meeting the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of (insert number) years after the end of the term of this Agreement, the Recipient is not to attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contracts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient for the purpose of circumventing, the result of which shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise, without the specific written app roval of the Owner. If any circumvention is to occur, the Owner is to be entitled to any commission due pursuant to this Agreement, or relating to such transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is not to attempt to do business with, or otherwise solicit any business contracts found or otherwise referred by Owner to Recipient. If any circumvention is to occur, the Owner is to be entitled to any commission due pursuant to this Agreement, or relating to such transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'For a period of (insert number) years after the end of the term of this Agreement... without the specific written approval of the Owner' and 'for the purpose of circumventing, the result of which shall be to prevent the Owner from realizing or recognizing a profit, fees, or otherwise' the modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Without a defined time period, the NON-CIRCUMVENTION clause becomes overly broad. This creates a conflict with the 'RELEVANT TERM' section which specifies a duration for the agreement's obligations, making it unclear which time period applies to non-circumvention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RELEVANT TERM The obligations of this Agreement are to survive 2 years form the effective date or until the owner sends the Recipient written notice releasing the Recipient from this Agreement. After that, the Recipient is to continue to protect the information received during the term of this Agreement from unauthorized use or disclosure indefinitely .
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the time limit and purpose related to non-circumvention, making it overly broad. This directly contradicts the 'RELEVANT TERM' section, which specifies a limited duration (2 years) for the agreement's obligations. The changed text implies non-circumvention is perpetual, creating a clear conflict in the contract's duration, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient understands the implied limitation of offering products or services created by the Recipient resulting from misuse of the received confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient understands the limitation of offering products or services.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'created by the Recipient resulting from misuse of the received confidential information' weakens the clause. It contradicts the intent of the 'LICENSING PROVISIONS' section. The original clause links limitations to misuse of confidential information, protecting the Owner's IP. The modified clause removes this link, potentially allowing the Recipient to argue that any product restriction is invalid regardless of whether confidential information was misused, conflicting with the spirit of IP protection outlined in LICENSING PROVISIONS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LICENSING PROVISIONS. The recipient shall not acquire any intellectual property rights under this Agreement, except the right to limited used of information as set forth and specified above. The Recipient acknowledges and understands that, as between the Owner and the Recipient , the informaiton and all related intellectual property rights are, and shall, at all times, be, the property of the Owner, even if suggestions, comments, or ideas made by the Recipient are incorporated into the confidential information or related material s during the vali dity of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited restrictions to situations involving misuse of confidential information. The changed text broadens the restriction to include offering any products or services, regardless of whether confidential information was misused. This contradicts the 'LICENSING PROVISIONS' section because it potentially allows the Owner to restrict any product, regardless of misuse, which conflicts with the original intent of limiting the restriction to products resulting from misuse of Confidential Information. The 'LICENSING PROVISIONS' section grants limited use and acknowledges the Owner's IP rights. The changed text could undermine this by allowing the Recipient to argue against IP restrictions even without misuse, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the experience, know -how, training and other vital organizational or market knowledge, which is to be acquired through working for the Owner, the Employee hereby promises that he/she will NOT, either during the employment or for a period of time of (insert number of years – usually up to two years ) consecutive years (months) after termination of employment, directly or indirectly, for the sake of the Employee or for any third party, accept employment or in any other way, directly or indirectly, engage in business activities which are of direct competition to the Owner. In this period , the Recipient further promises NOT TO solicit any current or potential customer of the Owner identified in the duration of the employment with the Owner, or otherwise divert, or attempt to do so , any business from the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Employee hereby promises that he/she will NOT, either during the employment, directly or indirectly, accept employment or in any other way, directly or indirectly, engage in business activities which are of direct competition to the Owner. The Recipient further promises NOT TO solicit any current or potential customer of the Owner, or otherwise divert, or attempt to do so, any business from the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'In consideration of the experience, know -how, training and other vital organizational or market knowledge, which is to be acquired through working for the Owner' and 'or for a period of time of (insert number of years – usually up to two years ) consecutive years (months) after termination of employment... identified in the duration of the employment with the Owner', we weaken the non-solicitation and non-competition clause. This creates a contradiction with the 'DAMAGES' section. Without a defined duration for the non-compete agreement, it becomes difficult to prove damages resulting from its breach, making the 'DAMAGES' section less enforceable since the scope of the restriction is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DAMAGES If damages, resulting as a breach of Terms on behalf of the Recipient cannot be determined , the Owne r has the right, in his/her sole discretion, to seek legal remedy amounting in the maximum amount of (set maximum amount), including all reasonable litigation expenses, court costs and reasonable attorney ‘s fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the defined period for the non-compete and non-solicitation clauses, specifically 'or for a period of time of (insert number of years – usually up to two years ) consecutive years (months) after termination of employment... identified in the duration of the employment with the Owner'. This creates a contradiction with the 'DAMAGES' section, as the 'DAMAGES' section relies on the breach of these terms. With no defined duration, it is difficult or impossible to quantify damages, making the 'DAMAGES' clause less enforceable. The original text sets a timeframe and consideration, which the changed text removes, creating a direct conflict with the 'DAMAGES' section that depends on a quantifiable breach.
